pages.raw_text:
Tonight delegations from both the
trades council and the typographical
union will be present at the city
council meeting to note the action
taken by each individual councilman
and to report to their respective
bodies whether these councilmen are
friendly or unfriendly to organized
labor.


It is the concensus Of opinion
among these delegations that imme-
date action should be taken by the
council, and the contracts be let to-
night.


The usual course Of action is to re-
fer the bids to the finance committee,
where action is taken upon them at
the committees leisure. Labor lead
ers assert, however. that the city's
interests demand immediate action.
"Whoever gets the contract" they
assert, "the price will be less than
the present exorbitant figures, for
there Is now competition. AS the
present contract remains in force UNI
til the new contract is let, 1t is q
matter Of economy to the city and
therefore to the taxpayers. that i'm
mediate action be taken"
